description:
About Perilya Limited

Perilya Limited is a well-established Australian mining company with a proud history and a strong international footprint. Our operations include the iconic Broken Hill Operation in New South Wales - one of the world’s great base metals mines with over 130 years of continuous production, and Coridom in the Dominican Republic, the country’s first underground mining operation, which continues to deliver high-quality zinc, lead, copper, and silver.

Together, these assets form the foundation of a business with scale, resilience, and long-term growth opportunities.

Perilya is recognised for its commitment to operational excellence, safety leadership, and environmental responsibility. We invest in our people and communities, fostering diversity and creating genuine career pathways across both corporate and operational sites. Our collaborative culture values integrity, accountability, and continuous improvement, making Perilya a workplace where talented people can thrive and contribute to a sustainable future in mining.

Role Details

We are seeking an experienced Data Engineer to join our Information Technology team and play a key role in building and optimising Perilya’s enterprise data platform.

In this role, you’ll design, build and maintain scalable data pipelines, data lakes and data warehouses that underpin reporting, business intelligence and advanced analytics across our operations. Working closely with Data Analysts, Data Architects and site stakeholders, you’ll help ensure our data is reliable, accessible and ready to support informed decision‑making across the business.

This role is well suited to someone who enjoys hands‑on engineering, problem solving, and collaborating across technical and operational teams in a mining/industrial environment.

Be advised this is a 6-month contract, based in our Adelaide Head Office.

What You’ll Be Doing

Design, build and maintain scalable ETL/ELT pipelines to ingest data from multiple source systems

Develop and manage data lakes and data warehouses, ensuring performance, security and reliability

Automate data ingestion and transformation to support both real‑time and batch processing

Implement data quality, validation, cleansing and reconciliation processes within pipelines

Design and execute data testing strategies across the full data lifecycle

Establish monitoring and alerting to proactively identify data quality or pipeline issues

Collaborate with Data Analysts to optimise data models for reporting and visualisation

Work with the Data Architect to support data governance and ensure architectural alignment

Provide technical support and subject matter expertise for data platforms and pipelines

Create and maintain clear technical documentation

Communicate technical concepts to both technical and non‑technical stakeholders

Travel to Perilya sites as required for project work

What You Will Bring

You are a motivated Data Engineer with strong technical capability and a practical, solutions‑focused mindset. You take pride in building reliable, well‑tested data solutions and enjoy working collaboratively across teams.

Key Skills & Experience

Essential:

Bachelor’s degree in Computer Science, Information Systems or a related discipline (or equivalent experience)

4+ years’ experience in a Data Engineering or similar role

Proven experience building and maintaining ETL/ELT pipelines

Strong proficiency in SQL and data warehousing concepts

Hands‑on experience with Microsoft Azure, including tools such as Azure Data Factory, Azure Synapse Analytics and Databricks

Strong programming experience using Python or Java

Excellent analytical and problem‑solving skills

Desirable:

Experience in the mining or industrial sector

Experience with Big Data technologies such as Spark or Hadoop

Relevant certifications (e.g. Microsoft Certified: Azure Data Engineer Associate)

Why Join Perilya

Work on enterprise‑wide data initiatives with real operational impact

Use modern Azure‑based data technologies, including Databricks

Collaborate with a supportive IT team across multiple sites and regions

Enjoy a stable, permanent role in an established organisation

Design and build scalable data pipelines, data lakes and warehouses in a complex, real‑world environment

How to Apply

To be considered for this role, please submit a brief covering letter and CV by following the APPLY NOW prompts.

At Perilya Limited, we value diversity and inclusion, strongly encouraging applications from women, Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander peoples, and candidates from culturally diverse backgrounds.

We are committed to building an inclusive workforce where everyone can contribute, grow, and succeed.
